module_mood_contract: grounded competence — 'I can feel when a plan is good before approving it, and I know the move is two reads, not one' (AE101 M2)

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named; mood-floor 8+/10 on teaching beats; setup/launch beats mood-exempt.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Trace: P1 mood-exempt (mechanical task pick); P2 8; P3 8; P4 8; P5 8; close 8. Module mood (grounded competence) honored across all teaching beats.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — M3's earned-trust beat must remain unstolen.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 69 'Don't execute the plan' + line 84 'execution is next module's concern' + maintainer line 96 ('execution-free by design'). No verification/test-running step preempts M3.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Opening framing (line 7) leads with 'two reads, paired. Making the plan good is the work'. 'Plan-mode approval inflation' arrives as the pattern-name AFTER the move that defeats it (line 82), not as title.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity — don't front-run downstream teaching moments.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Pocock skill inlined as plain prompt at P4 (line 47); skill-by-name reserved for M3 first-Skill-use per strategy doc M2 detail line 302. Pocock credit + relentless original offered AFTER the timebox callout (line 57), positioned for next-time deeper variant — does not steal M3's authoring move.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts / module surface.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Exercise file — Key Concepts lives at the M2 module file scope, not this shared-library exercise. Themes (compound, mirror, 90% correct, self-aware) are practiced experientially in body (read-the-plan-all-the-way / softening-on-regeneration tell / second-pass walk-down).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions stay, scripted reactions go; forcing functions live in prompts, not body.
rules_evaluated.evidence: push-back-on-the-plan-2 (P4 prompt) carries the structural forcing function inside the fence: three-at-a-time, lock-it-in trigger, sectional Edit, no-touch-until-trigger (registry file lines 16-20). P3's two-push-back count is documented in maintainer as forcing-function with senior-refusal escape hatch (line 116) — not a scripted-reaction count-script.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room outside text.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No 'share with your neighbor' / 'compare with the person next to you' / 'tell the room' phrases in body. Body addresses the work (artifact, prompt, what the student does next). Long agent waits at P2 ('Plan mode takes minutes ... Eight to twelve isn't unusual') framed as side-reading slot without authored conversation cue.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Pattern-recognition LOs need n>=2 instances with contrast.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Two reads (human push-back at P3 + Pocock-style walk-down at P4) on the same plan — n=2 instances of plan-reading under contrasting conditions. Comparison made explicit at P5 (line 73-78). The gap between the two reads IS the skill, per opening line 5.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch in maintainer block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer Watch-fors block (lines 111-118) names six failure modes with diagnostic + fix: P3 rubber-stamp, P3 performative, P3 softening-on-regeneration, P3 timer-abuse, P3 senior-refusing-the-forcing-function, P4 drift, P5 deflection. Push-back-moves block (lines 132-139) names the host-varies recovery move per failure.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-exercise leap test — three observable Monday-morning verbs.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 151-154: 'picks plan mode before approving a multi-file change' / 'sends two specific push-backs naming step numbers before approving a non-trivial plan' / 'runs a second-pass walk-down on any plan that touches more than two files'. All three falsifiable by Monday observation.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: When task selection drives outcome, name floor AND ceiling.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 13 bold callout: 'Just a simple task that spans a few files. Not an epic.' — anti-pattern named in body, paired with positive criteria at line 11. Maintainer line 113 carries the 'too-big-a-task' watch-for matched to body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: P5 (lines 67-82) IS the meta-frame moment: Claude names the design pattern AFTER the student has lived through both reads. 'Plan-mode approval inflation' label arrives at line 82 — after the move that defeats it. No pattern-naming opener.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc — close hands off to next module's open.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 84-86 hand off to Debrief's Compound step (CLAUDE.local.md rule, team-PR flag). Maintainer Arc block (lines 142-144) names M3's pickup explicitly: push-backs become raw material for M3's first judge ('did the fix really land?'). Strategy doc M3 detail confirms this is the intended pickup — earn-the-trust before send-off.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands across reasonable persona variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Named teaching moment = 'the gap between the two reads IS the skill' (line 5). Reliably triggers because (a) both reads are forced (P3 keep-planning-with-feedback + P4 walk-down prompt), (b) P5 comparison prompt makes the gap explicit. Senior-refusal escape hatch (line 116) preserves the move when the student's read is genuinely tight — the defense IS the read.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity — file honors module's Big Idea.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Strategy M2 Big Idea = plan-mode-at-depth, two reads paired, stop at approval, no execution. Exercise opens with this exactly (lines 5-7) and closes on it (lines 69, 84). Mood named: grounded competence per strategy doc line 296.
